message:
fix(websocket): validate close codes on parse and tighten RFC 6455 wire-sendable set

CloseReason::parse() now rejects reserved and unassigned status codes at the
wire boundary using CloseCode::is_valid_code(), rather than blindly accepting
any u16. This prevents propagation of codes like 1004 (reserved), 1005/1006
(internal-only), and unassigned ranges that have no defined semantics.

Frame::close() panic assertion upgraded from a hand-rolled 3-code blocklist
(1005, 1006, 1015) to the centralized is_valid_code() check, catching
additional invalid codes like 1004 and unassigned values in the 1012-1015
range.

CloseCode::is_sendable() now also excludes Reserved (1004) from the set of
codes that may appear on the wire.

CloseHandshake::receive_close() echo path changed from converting the peer
code through CloseCode (which normalizes unknown codes) back to reading the
raw u16 from the frame payload. This preserves custom application codes in
the 3000-4999 range exactly as received.

Added tests:
- close_reason_parse_invalid_reserved_code (1004)
- close_reason_parse_invalid_unassigned_code (1012)
- handshake_receive_close_echoes_custom_code (3001)
- close_frame_code_1004_panics
- close_frame_code_1012_panics
- Reserved included in is_sendable() == false assertions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
